---
title: "Walk-Forward Evaluation Results"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: show
execute:
  warning: false
---

# Goal

Headline results from the walk-forward backtest: model vs baselines across
all folds, lead-time distribution for true positives, and a per-fold
stability check.

# Inputs

This notebook reads the artifacts produced by `evaluation.runner.run_evaluation`.
Persist them once and re-run this notebook to refresh the report:

```python
from evaluation.runner import run_evaluation
out = run_evaluation(cutoffs, spec, funding_events)
out["per_fold"].to_parquet("eval_per_fold.parquet")
out["summary"].to_parquet("eval_summary.parquet")
out["scores"].to_parquet("eval_scores.parquet")
```

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from evaluation.metrics import median_lead_time
```

# Headline table

Mean (std) of each metric across folds, broken out by model.

```{python}
#| eval: false
PER_FOLD = "eval_per_fold.parquet"
SUMMARY = "eval_summary.parquet"

per_fold = pd.read_parquet(PER_FOLD)
summary = pd.read_parquet(SUMMARY)

headline_metrics = ["pr_auc", "precision_at_10", "precision_at_20", "lift_at_10", "recall_at_50"]
headline = summary[summary["metric"].isin(headline_metrics)].copy()
headline["cell"] = headline.apply(lambda r: f"{r['mean']:.3f} ± {r['std']:.3f}", axis=1)
table = headline.pivot(index="model", columns="metric", values="cell")
table = table[headline_metrics]
table
```

# Lead-time distribution

For true positives, how many days before the round did the model first
score them above threshold?

```{python}
#| eval: false
SCORES = "eval_scores.parquet"
scores = pd.read_parquet(SCORES)
xgb = scores[scores["model"] == "xgboost"]

# True funding dates from the labels frame are reconstructable from the
# scores frame: companies with label == 1 have an actual round in the
# label window; you'll typically join in the source funding_events frame
# to get the exact round date. For this notebook we assume a parquet
# `funding_events.parquet` with company_id, round_date.
funding = pd.read_parquet("funding_events.parquet")
true_positives = xgb[xgb["label"] == 1]["company_id"].unique()
fd = funding[funding["company_id"].isin(true_positives)].set_index("company_id")["round_date"]

# threshold = score of the 20th-ranked company on the most recent fold
last_cut = xgb["as_of_date"].max()
threshold = xgb[xgb["as_of_date"] == last_cut].nlargest(20, "score")["score"].min()

lead = median_lead_time(xgb, fd, threshold=threshold)
print(f"Median lead time @ top-20 threshold: {lead:.1f} days")

# distribution plot
gh = xgb[xgb["score"] >= threshold].groupby("company_id")["as_of_date"].min()
joined = gh.to_frame("first_above").join(fd, how="inner")
joined = joined[joined["first_above"] <= joined["round_date"]]
leads = (joined["round_date"] - joined["first_above"]).dt.days

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))
ax.hist(leads, bins=15, edgecolor="black")
ax.axvline(leads.median(), linestyle="--", color="red", label=f"median = {leads.median():.0f}d")
ax.set_xlabel("Days between first above-threshold score and funding event")
ax.set_ylabel("True positives")
ax.legend(); ax.set_title("Lead-time distribution")
plt.show()
```

# Per-fold stability

Plots metric value as a function of cutoff date. Stable models have flat
curves; brittle models have curves that swing with the macro environment.

```{python}
#| eval: false
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4), sharey=False)
for ax, metric in zip(axes, ["pr_auc", "precision_at_20"]):
    pivot = per_fold[per_fold["metric"] == metric].pivot(
        index="cutoff", columns="model", values="value"
    )
    pivot.plot(ax=ax, marker="o")
    ax.set_title(metric); ax.set_xlabel("Cutoff date"); ax.set_ylabel(metric)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

# Per-fold table

For diagnostic deep-dives.

```{python}
#| eval: false
diag = per_fold.pivot_table(
    index=["cutoff", "model"], columns="metric", values="value"
).reset_index()
diag.head(20)
```

# Stability summary

```{python}
#| eval: false
stab = (
    per_fold.groupby(["model", "metric"])["value"]
    .agg(["mean", "std", "min", "max"])
    .reset_index()
)
stab["coef_var"] = stab["std"] / stab["mean"].replace(0, np.nan)
stab[stab["metric"].isin(["pr_auc", "precision_at_20"])].sort_values(["metric", "coef_var"])
```

# Takeaways checklist

- [ ] XGBoost beats every baseline on `precision_at_10`/`lift_at_10`?
- [ ] Lead time median is large enough to be actionable for outreach?
- [ ] No fold is catastrophically bad (within ~2x of the average)?
- [ ] The recency baseline isn't already capturing most of the lift?
